hotspots.title: Frontier labs tighten inference SLAs for enterprise
hotspots.summary: Several providers published latency and uptime commitments aimed at regulated buyers, signaling a shift from raw capability claims to operational guarantees.

hotspots.title: Open-weight tooling gains traction in on-prem workflows
hotspots.summary: Teams are mixing small local models with retrieval and policy layers; the story is less about one model and more about composable stacks.

hotspots.title: Evaluation harnesses move toward task suites, not single scores
hotspots.summary: Benchmarks framed as scenario batteries are replacing leaderboard chasing; buyers want reproducible passes on their own data slices.

deep_dive.title: Why “agent reliability” is becoming a procurement keyword
deep_dive.narrative: Enterprises are asking for failure modes, rollback paths, and human-in-the-loop defaults before expanding automations. Vendors that document operational playbooks are winning pilots; those that only demo demos are stalled in PoC.

trends.title: Retrieval quality as the bottleneck
trends.summary: Chunking, freshness, and permission-aware indexes matter more than marginal model upgrades for many internal copilots.

trends.title: Synthetic data disclosure
trends.summary: Expect more labeling of training and eval provenance as procurement and compliance teams join the conversation.

trends.title: Purely cloud-only AI stacks
trends.summary: Air-gapped and hybrid deployments are pulling budget and attention away from default public API paths.

risks_opportunities.title: Shadow AI spend
risks_opportunities.detail: Department-level tools without centralized review may create inconsistent data handling.

risks_opportunities.title: Evaluation as a shared service
risks_opportunities.detail: Central teams that own benchmarks and red-teaming can unblock multiple product lines at once.
